cases.title: Testing-plan §11 NFR-07 verification row — one success per student/session under retries
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.2 reliability tests and §11 NFR verification matrix row for NFR-07 apply.
Representative fixture: open class sessions with enrolled students, valid rotating QR tokens, and satisfied authentication and policy preconditions.
Test harness can submit retries (same Idempotency-Key replays and distinct-key duplicate attempts) and count AttendanceRecord rows per (studentUserId, classSessionId).
steps: For each enrolled student in the pilot cohort, execute an initial successful POST /v1/check-ins.
Replay each success with identical Idempotency-Key, actor, and qrToken per testing-plan §7.2 retry-storm profile.
Submit additional duplicate attempts with new Idempotency-Key values and fresh valid qrTokens for the same student-session pairs.
Query attendance_records and aggregate row counts per (studentUserId, classSessionId) across all retry and duplicate paths.
cases.expected: At most one successful AttendanceRecord exists per (studentUserId, classSessionId) after all retries and duplicate submissions; idempotent replays return prior success without a second ledger row; new-key duplicates return DuplicateCheckIn conflict, satisfying NFR-07 and linked AC-08 per testing-plan §11 minimum evidence.

cases.title: Attendance ledger DB unique key enforces one success record per student-session pair
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for attendance_records and check_in_attempts tables.
Domain model DI-04 unique constraint on (class_session_id, student_user_id) is active.
Open session with valid QR token exists; authenticated enrolled student has no prior success for the session.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, Idempotency-Key K1, and student bearer token; verify Success and persisted AttendanceRecord.
Submit duplicate POST /v1/check-ins with new Idempotency-Key K2 and fresh valid qrToken for the same student-session.
Query attendance_records filtered by (classSessionId, studentUserId) and verify row count and field immutability.
Inspect check_in_attempts for both submissions including failed duplicate outcome.
cases.expected: Exactly one AttendanceRecord row persists for the student-session pair; duplicate command persists a failed CheckInAttempt with DuplicateCheckIn without mutating the original success row; DB unique key and service-layer guard both enforce NFR-07 one-success invariant.

cases.title: Retry storm with same Idempotency-Key after success does not create duplicate attendance rows
preconditions: Student has completed one successful POST /v1/check-ins for an Open session with Idempotency-Key K1.
Idempotency store retains the accepted command for (K1, actor, token) within replay window per API design §7.1.
Test harness can fire 10+ parallel replay requests with identical K1, bearer token, and qrToken.
steps: Fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins replays with identical Idempotency-Key K1, same actor bearer token, and same qrToken as the accepted success.
Collect all HTTP responses and inspect data.outcome and error.code.
Query attendance_records for (student, session) and count rows; query check_in_attempts for new rows attributable to replays.
cases.expected: All idempotent replays return HTTP 200 with prior success summary; no additional AttendanceRecord rows are created; retry storm under unstable network conditions preserves exactly one success per student/session per NFR-07 and testing-plan §7.2.

cases.title: Class-start concurrent burst preserves one success per student/session across parallel first attempts and post-success duplicates
preconditions: Multiple open class sessions each have 20+ enrolled students per representative pilot profile.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins across sessions with distinct students, valid QR tokens, and unique Idempotency-Key values (performance-smoke profile).
After burst first-attempt phase completes, harness submits duplicate attempts with new keys for students who succeeded.
steps: Trigger simultaneous first-attempt POST /v1/check-ins for distinct enrolled students across concurrent open sessions mimicking class-start burst.
Verify each (studentUserId, classSessionId) pair has at most one Success AttendanceRecord after the burst settles.
For a sample of students with success, fire concurrent duplicate POST /v1/check-ins with distinct Idempotency-Key values and fresh valid qrTokens.
Aggregate attendance_records row counts per student-session across the full burst and duplicate phase.
cases.expected: Under class-start concurrency, exactly one successful AttendanceRecord exists per (studentUserId, classSessionId); concurrent post-success duplicates all return DuplicateCheckIn without extra success rows; idempotency invariant holds at peak load per NFR-07, NFR-04, and testing-plan §5.2 data integrity checks.

cases.title: In-flight check-in and session close overlap preserves one success and idempotent close semantics
preconditions: Open class session exists with enrolled student who has no prior success.
Lecturer bearer token is available for POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Test harness can submit overlapping POST /v1/check-ins and close command with distinct Idempotency-Key values.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins from enrolled student with valid token while close command is in flight or immediately before close.
POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close; verify session state Closed.
Replay close command with same Idempotency-Key; verify idempotent closed response per state machine SMI-02.
Query AttendanceRecord count for the student-session pair and attempt post-close duplicate check-in.
cases.expected: At most one successful AttendanceRecord exists for the student-session pair; close replay returns idempotent closed metadata without double finalization; post-close check-in returns SessionClosed not a second success; NFR-07 one-success invariant holds through session lifecycle overlap per testing-plan §7.2 and state machines §3.3.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins idempotent replay returns HTTP 200; new-key duplicate returns HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token has no prior success for the session.
E2E harness records HTTP status, envelope shape (data/meta/error), and persisted row counts.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key K1; verify HTTP 200, data.outcome Success, and data.attendanceStatus Present or Late.
Replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical K1, same actor, and same qrToken; inspect HTTP status and response body.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with new Idempotency-Key K2 and fresh valid qrToken for the same student-session.
Verify AttendanceRecord row count for (student, session) remains exactly one.
cases.expected: Idempotency replay of K1 returns HTTP 200 with prior success summary without second AttendanceRecord; new-key duplicate returns HTTP 409 with error.code DuplicateCheckIn and data null; API contract enforces NFR-07 one-success-per-session invariant per API design §5.1 and §7.1.

cases.title: E2E-03 alternate workflow branch — student duplicate submission preserves original success record
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session (AC-01) and session is Open with rotating QR.
Student completes login gate (AC-06), enrollment validation (AC-07), and first successful check-in yielding Present or Late (AC-11).
Fresh valid QR token is available for a second submission attempt.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and Idempotency-Key K1; record first success outcome and checkInAt timestamp T1.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins again with a new valid qrToken and Idempotency-Key K2.
Lecturer queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for the student row.
Replay first submission with Idempotency-Key K1 and original qrToken.
cases.expected: Second submission fails with DuplicateCheckIn (409); roster shows unchanged single Present/Late row with original checkInAt T1; K1 replay returns HTTP 200 prior success summary; no extra success AttendanceRecord is created, satisfying NFR-07 and testing-plan E2E-03.

cases.title: Concurrent first-success race for same student-session yields exactly one AttendanceRecord
preconditions: Open session with valid QR token exists; enrolled student has no prior success for the session.
Test harness can fire two or more parallel POST /v1/check-ins with different Idempotency-Key values and the same valid qrToken before any success commits.
Harness captures all HTTP outcomes and final DB state.
steps: Fire concurrent POST /v1/check-ins requests for the same student-session pair with distinct Idempotency-Key values and identical valid qrToken.
Wait for all responses; classify outcomes as Success or DuplicateCheckIn/conflict.
Query attendance_records for (student, session) and count rows with Success-equivalent status.
Replay the winning Idempotency-Key and verify idempotent 200 response.
cases.expected: Exactly one successful AttendanceRecord is committed; concurrent losers return DuplicateCheckIn or deterministic conflict without a second success row; winning-key replay returns prior success summary; first-success-wins semantics per state machines §6.2 and NFR-07 hold under race conditions.

cases.title: FLOW-02 mobile-web duplicate check-in journey shows localized info alert without retry CTA
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web viewport (Chromium mobile emulation) with open session and valid check-in deep link.
Student has already completed successful check-in for the target Open session (Present or Late).
Fresh valid QR token is available via deep link for a second submission attempt.
steps: Navigate to /check-in?token=... with valid token for the already-checked-in session.
Submit check-in from the student check-in form.
Observe CheckInResultScreen failure-duplicate info alert styling and Vietnamese localized copy per NFR-14.
Verify absence of retry CTA and display of existing check-in status and timestamp.
Refresh page and resubmit; confirm UI still shows duplicate guidance without creating a second success state in roster API.
cases.expected: UI renders failure-duplicate info alert with localized Vietnamese message (e.g. 'Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi.'); no retry button is shown; student sees existing success status and timestamp; backend retains exactly one success AttendanceRecord per NFR-07.

cases.title: Physical mobile retry behavior under campus network instability preserves one success per session
preconditions: Staging pilot uses real iOS Safari or Android Chrome on campus Wi-Fi per testing-plan §6.2 browser matrix.
Open session with valid QR; enrolled student has not yet checked in.
Campus network conditions may cause client-side retries; physical device matrix is not reproducible in Playwright MCP harness.
steps: Student scans classroom QR on physical mobile device and submits check-in.
If network stalls, student retries submission (browser or client retry) before seeing confirmation.
Verify UI eventually shows single success confirmation with one timestamp.
Student attempts second scan/submit; verify duplicate info alert and unchanged single success in lecturer roster.
cases.expected: Despite network retries on physical device, at most one successful attendance record exists per student-session; duplicate UI surfaces correctly on second intentional attempt; provides production-like NFR-07 evidence beyond emulated harness runs.
